title: Warframe

notes: Seemed to work well at first. Used the fix found at https://gitlab.com/GloriousEggroll/warframe-linux/tree/steamplay-proton/ which allowed it to run. Have to close an empty DOS window to get it going. Was able to join a group without issue. Noticed that lighting was rather bright. Used Nidus ability 1 and the game crashed. Might be just a Nidus issue. Was working fine up to that point.
title: Warframe

notes:
Follow directions here: https://gitlab.com/GloriousEggroll/warframe-linux/tree/steamplay-proton

Only reason I am giving it a silver instead of gold is because the Proton ends the game after 5 minutes if no controller is plugged in.

The Lutris version of Warframe = Platinum so if Valve took a look at this and the work Glorious Eggroll as put into make it run, they can get it to Platinum easily!

title: Warframe

notes:
The game won't launch unless you're using the launcher fix found here: https://gitlab.com/GloriousEggroll/warframe-linux/tree/steamplay-proton

Performance is great, input works fine and there are no noticeable gameplay / visual glitches. Only thing to keep in mind is that you need to plug in a game controller or else Proton will exit the game.

I'm rating this 'Silver' because the audio tends to break after about 30-60 mins of playing the game. It starts to distort and is generally unpleasant to listen to once it starts happening. The only fix I know of so far is to restart the game every time this happens. Minor issue, but still annoying.

notes: Doesn't start in current Proton.  Yes, you can make it work with GloriousEggroll fixes and launcher, but it uses completely different version of wine, patched binaries, and and uses a lot tricks, that is most likely going to break with time (i.e. once game is updated) or break other games. You are better off playing it without Steam.

notes: Crash on startup.
title: Warframe

notes:
1. Install/Update GloriousEggroll's workaround/patch (Now with 64bit client support, Fortuna update crashes -mostly- fixed.
 
https://gitlab.com/GloriousEggroll/warframe-linux/tree/steamplay-proton

2. Set launch Options
SDL_AUDIODRIVER=directsound %command%

Alt-tabbing might produce a crackling/buzz noise, repeat alt-tabbing to get the audio fixed.

notes: https://gitlab.com/GloriousEggroll/warframe-linux/tree/steamplay-proton   /// SteamController connected but not used ingame
title: Warframe

notes:
You have to use GloriousEggroll's launcher to get the game to run: (https://gitlab.com/GloriousEggroll/warframe-linux/tree/steamplay-proton)


To stop the game force closing after 5mins either have a controller plugged in or install xboxdrv and run: (xboxdrv -D) in the terminal before starting the game.


To stop the audio breaking turn off the music in game from the settings.


Played the game for over 18hours and it runs flawlessly otherwise.
title: Warframe

notes: The Glorious Eggroll script is required. Video and framerate are great. Audio buzzes a lot, even with some suggested tweaks. This can make it uncomfortable to play, but otherwise runs great.

Crashes are rare now that GloriousEggroll's patches use FAudio, but there is a noticable buzzing that develops over time (which can be treated by muting and unmuting the game; or entering and exiting the menu in Solo mode). DO NOT USE LUTRIS - it currently broken for warframe

title: Warframe

notes: I have tried numerous tweaks and ways of making this game stable, will run but low performance and crashes randomly.
title: Warframe

notes:
*Almost* works out of the box on the 3.16-6 proton beta. There will be some missing audio, which you can resolve by executing the following in your terminal:  
  
WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/230410/pfx/ winetricks xact    
  
(Install winetricks if necessary)  
No crashes, the occasional frame drops occurred in situations identical to running the game through windows. If migrating from a windows install, note that you can copy your keybindings from AppData/Local/Warframe/EE.cfg in windows to the following in linux:
Steam/steamapps/compatdata/230410/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local Settings/Application Data/Warframe/EE.cfg
title: Warframe

notes:
I've had crashes due to not having controller plugged in. That was resolved by installing xboxdrv driver. With that warframe runs almost perfectly, only issues are mute NPCs and image stuttering during mission loading (haven't noticed any during gameplay).  Performance is good, fps comparable to what I have on windows.
(Gloriouseggrolls launcher not used)
